content.problemStatement:
Given an integer NNN, let


f(N)f(N)f(N) denote the number of even divisors of NNN
g(N)g(N)g(N) denote the number of odd divisors of NNN.
For example, the divisors of 666 are 1,2,31, 2, 31,2,3 and 666. f(N)=2,g(N)=2f(N) = 2, g(N) = 2f(N)=2,g(N)=2 as there are 222 even and 222 odd numbers in this list;.

Find whether f(N)>g(N)f(N) > g(N)f(N)>g(N) or f(N)=g(N)f(N) = g(N)f(N)=g(N) or f(N)<g(N)f(N) < g(N)f(N)<g(N).

Output


111 if f(N)>g(N)f(N) > g(N)f(N)>g(N)
000 if f(N)=g(N)f(N) = g(N)f(N)=g(N)
−1-1−1 if f(N)<g(N)f(N) < g(N)f(N)<g(N)

Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line consists of a single integer NNN.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line :



111 if f(N)>g(N)f(N) > g(N)f(N)>g(N)
000 if f(N)=g(N)f(N) = g(N)f(N)=g(N)
−1-1−1 if f(N)<g(N)f(N) < g(N)f(N)<g(N)

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : 111 has only 111 divisor, which is odd. Hence f(1)=0,g(1)=1f(1) = 0, g(1) = 1f(1)=0,g(1)=1, and we can see f(1)<g(1)f(1) < g(1)f(1)<g(1).
Test Case 2 : 222 has 222 factors - 111 and 222. Hence, f(2)=1f(2) = 1f(2)=1, g(2)=1g(2) = 1g(2)=1 and we see f(2)=g(2)f(2) = g(2)f(2)=g(2).
